member.name: Gordon McKee
member.synopsis: Gordon McKee is the Labour MP for Glasgow South , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to ensure that the Great British Railways brand supports a unified national rail network across Great Britain.

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what estimate he has made of the number of jobs in Glasgow supported by contracts for the Royal Navy Type-26 frigate programme.

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Ukrainian personnel have completed military training in the UK under UK-led programmes.

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether the UK plans to nominate a candidate for a seat on the European Committee of Social Rights at the Council of Europe.

writtenQuestions.heading: Film and Television: Glasgow
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to help support film and television production in Glasgow.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to growing the UK screen sector across all nations. Glasgow, identified as a high-potential creative cluster in the Creative Industries Sector Plan, is well placed to benefit from the Government's £75 million Screen Growth Pack...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: WiFi
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to help improve the reliability of Wi-Fi on long-distance rail services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has secured funding to support the introduction of low earth orbit satellite connectivity on mainline trains, which is expected to deliver a significant improvement in the reliability and performance of onboard Wi‑Fi for passe...

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Right hon. Member for Kenilworth and Southam, representing the Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission, what steps the Electoral Commission is taking to protect elections from foreign interference.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Elections in the UK continue to be well-run, but the Electoral Commission has highlighted that the electoral system faces growing threats, including from foreign interference.The Commission’s regulatory remit is focused on ensuring the safeguards against ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Speaker's Committee on the Electoral Commission

writtenQuestions.heading: Written question
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Scotland, what recent discussions he has had with the Scottish Government on the devolving of power to local authorities.

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration: Glasgow
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what face-to-face support is available to people in accessing digital immigration status in Glasgow.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: Glasgow South
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department has taken to make railway stations in Glasgow South constituency accessible.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Future funding for the Access for All programme will be considered as part of the next Spending Review, which may provide an opportunity for accessibility upgrades at stations in the Glasgow South constituency. The process and timings for future funding r...

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Modular Reactors: Rolls-Royce
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking with Rolls-Royce to maximise the role of domestic manufacturing capability within the Small Modular Reactor programme supply chain.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Great British Energy – Nuclear (GBE-N) has an ambition that 70% of supply chain products will be British built across the small modular reactor (SMR) fleet, including both on-site and off-site activity. We intend for this ambition to be met.Now that GBE-N...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Tests: Glasgow
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to reduce waiting times for driving tests in Glasgow.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency (DVSA) is continuing with its recruitment campaigns for new driving examiners (DE) at driving test centres (DTC) serving the Glasgow area, to provide as many tests as possible. A full-time DE can be expected to add ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Euro 2028: Community Development
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to help ensure that hosting UEFA EURO 2028 supports communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Football reaches communities like nothing else. The department is working with partners to develop the largest-ever UEFA EURO Community Programme, backed by a social impact fund of around £45 million, which will ensure the impact supports communities far ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Ceasefires
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what diplomatic steps he is taking to support a ceasefire agreement in Gaza.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are clear that the first step to ending this terrible conflict is an immediate ceasefire, and we continue to push all parties to achieve this urgently. All parties must re-engage with negotiations to get the hostages out, surge aid, and secure a perman...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybersecurity: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much and what proportion of the increased defence budget will be spent on developing the UK's cyber offensive capabilities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: His Majesty's Treasury is currently undertaking the second phase of the Spending Review, which will be announced on 11 June 2025. This will set Departmental budgets for three years from 2026-27 for RDEL and four years for CDEL, including for the Ministry ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cryptoassets: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether her Department plans to bring forward legislative proposals to implement the cryptoasset regulatory framework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to fostering a vibrant, competitive, and innovative financial services sector. Last October, the Treasury published detailed proposals for the financial service regulation of cryptoassets and stablecoins. The Government has bee...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mandatory Provident Fund: British National (Overseas)
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will hold discussions with Standard Chartered on steps to help Hong Kongers with British National (Overseas) passports access their Mandatory Provident Fund (MPF) retirement savings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is deeply committed to supporting members of the Hong Kong community who have relocated to the UK. We are aware that individuals who have chosen to take up the British National (Overseas) route are having difficulties accessing their Manda...

writtenQuestions.heading: Andy Murray
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, if she will take steps to mark the retirement of Sir Andy Murray.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I am delighted to take this opportunity to wish Sir Andy well in his retirement. He has contributed a huge amount to British sport throughout his long career, both on and off the court.We know how important access to facilities is as part of the legacy of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many dentists his Department employs to provide care to military personnel in Scotland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As at 2 September 2024 the Ministry of Defence has nine dentists in permanent posts providing dental care to military personnel in Scotland. Temporary Healthcare Workers engaged by Defence Primary Healthcare (DPHC) provide additional capacity to meet dent...

writtenQuestions.heading: Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if she will implement the remaining provisions of Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Yes. Please see the King's Speech 2024: background briefing notes here: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/kings-speech-2024-background-briefing-notes.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
